product_updates.title: OpenAI launches new voice intelligence features in its API
product_updates.content: The new features could be handy for customer service systems, but OpenAI says they have applications that work across a variety of other fields, including education and creator platforms.

product_updates.title: Voi founders’ new AI startup Pit has become the latest rising star out of Stockholm
product_updates.content: AI startup Pit is led by the co-founders of European scooter giant Voi and backed by a16z, which is leading the startup’s $16 million seed round.

product_updates.title: OpenAI introduces new ‘Trusted Contact’ safeguard for cases of possible self-harm
product_updates.content: The company is expanding its efforts to protect ChatGPT users in cases where conversations may turn to self-harm.

product_updates.title: Perplexity’s Personal Computer is now available to everyone on Mac
product_updates.content: Perplexity's Personal Computer brings AI agents to your Mac, and is now open to everyone.

product_updates.title: Elon Musk’s lawsuit is putting OpenAI’s safety record under the microscope
product_updates.content: Elon Musk's legal effort to dismantle OpenAI may hinge on how its for-profit subsidiary enhances or detracts from the frontier lab's founding mission of ensuring that humanity benefits from artificial general intelligence.

product_updates.title: Bumble is getting rid of the swipe, CEO says
product_updates.content: Based on Whitney Wolfe Herd's past comments about Bumble's new direction, the company is expected to lean into AI -- Bumble is even working on an AI dating assistant called Bee, and the CEO has made many comments over the years about how AI will be "a supercharger to love and relationships."

product_updates.title: How Anthropic’s Mythos has rewritten Firefox’s approach to cybersecurity
product_updates.content: Security researchers at Mozilla say Anthropic's Mythos has unearthed a wealth of high-severity bugs in Firefox.

product_updates.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27: A shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K
product_updates.content: Startup Battlefield 200 applications are open, but only for three more weeks. Apply by May 27 for your shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, $100,000 equity-free, and more opportunities for major scaling impact.

product_updates.title: Exhibit at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Get in front of 10,000 decision-makers before space runs out
product_updates.content: If startup visibility, traction, and real deals matter, you should be on the TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 exhibit floor. Get your 6' exhibit table before your competitor does.

product_updates.title: Aurora’s Chris Urmson on why self-driving trucks are finally ready to scale
product_updates.content: Self-driving has been “almost here” for over a decade. But somewhere between DARPA challenges and a handful of driverless trucks hauling freight between Dallas and Houston, Aurora co-founder and CEO Chris Urmson’s story changed. The self-driving truck company started commercial driverless operati…

technical_breakthroughs.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
technical_breakthroughs.content: The developer of Firefox says it has "completely bought in" on AI-assisted bug discovery.

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: the tech reshaping IVF and the rise of balcony solar
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. What’s next for IVF IVF has brought millions of babies into the world over the last four decades. But the process can still be slow, painful, and expensive—and…

technical_breakthroughs.title: The balcony solar boom is coming to the US
technical_breakthroughs.content: Dozens of US states are considering legislation to allow people to install plug-in solar systems, often called balcony solar. These small arrays require little to no setup and could help cut emissions and power bills. Balcony solar is already popular in Europe, and proponents say that the systems…

technical_breakthroughs.title: What’s next for IVF
technical_breakthroughs.content: MIT Technology Review’s What’s Next series looks across industries, trends, and technologies to give you a first look at the future. You can read the rest of them here. Forty-eight years ago this July, Louise Joy Brown became the world’s first person born with the help of in vitro fertilization. …
